Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I should recall the standard steps in a P2P process From what I remember the typical flow is Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now I need to check each case 1001 to 1004 and see if the sequence of activities follows this standard flow Any deviations might be anomalies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Starting with Case 1001 The sequence looks good Create Requisition Approve Issue PO Receive Goods Invoice Received Match Release Payment Close That seems standard No issues here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Looking at the order Create Requisition Issue PO Wait the approval is missing here The next activity is Vendor Invoice Received Then Release Payment then Approve Requisition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The approval happened after the PO was issued and even after the invoice was received and payment was released Thats a problem Normally the requisition should be approved before issuing the PO Also releasing payment before approving the requisition is a big issue

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could lead to unauthorized purchases and payments without proper approval which is a risk for fraud or errors

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Sequence Create Requisition Approve Issue PO Release Payment Vendor Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Close Wait the payment was released before the vendor even sent the invoice and before receiving the goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thats a major anomaly Normally you receive the goods first then get the invoice match it and then pay

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Paying before receiving goods or the invoice is risky because you might pay for something you havent received leading to potential disputes or losses if the vendor doesnt deliver

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Sequence Create Requisition Approve Receive Goods Issue PO Vendor Invoice Received Match Release Payment Close Here the goods were received before the purchase order was issued

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thats not right The standard process is to issue the PO first then receive goods against that PO

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receiving goods without a PO could mean theres no official order leading to potential issues like unauthorized purchases incorrect items or payment problems since the invoice might not match a valid PO

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the main anomalies are in Cases 1002 1003 and 1004 Each has a different issue
